steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify angle x° and its relative sides
steps.content:
The angle $x^{\circ}$ is at the top-right corner. From this angle:
  • Opposite side = 40
  • Adjacent side = 39

steps.title: Use the tangent ratio to find c
steps.content:
Using opposite = 40 and adjacent = 39:
$$\tan x^{\circ} = \frac{40}{39}$$
Since we know $\tan x^{\circ} = \frac{c}{39}$, directly comparing gives $c = 40$. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
